title: Human Fracture Analysis Signal Definitions
purpose: Provides operational definitions for Human Fracture Analysis signals so that narrative detection can be evaluated consistently and explained transparently.

signals.name: Coercive Retention Signal
signals.description: Detected when a person tries to keep the other in the bond through guilt, pressure, fear, dependency, or captivity logic.
inclusion_criteria: Explicit attempt to stop departure by guilt pressure
Threats of collapse used to bind the other
Financial or housing control used to trap the other
Narrative framing where the other's freedom is treated as illegitimate
exclusion_criteria: Ordinary grief or pleading without coercive force
One-time expression of sadness without captivity logic
Practical negotiation about children or housing without pressure
signals.false_positive_risk: Strong emotional pleading may be misread as coercion when it remains non-captive in structure.
signals.escalation_threshold: Escalates to critical if combined with threats, financial trapping, surveillance, or child leverage.

signals.name: Degrading Speech Signal
signals.description: Detected when language strips the other of personhood, dignity, or moral standing.
inclusion_criteria: Insults aimed at humiliation
Objectifying or contemptuous language
Public shaming or narrative ridicule
Language framing the other as trash, enemy, vermin, or nothing
exclusion_criteria: Firm criticism of conduct without personhood attack
Private frustration stated without degrading labels
Accurate naming of harm without contempt style
signals.false_positive_risk: Heated language may look degrading even when the underlying statement is a conduct critique rather than person attack.
signals.escalation_threshold: Escalates to critical when public humiliation, repeated contempt, or child exposure is involved.

signals.name: Bitterness Identity Signal
signals.description: Detected when suffering becomes an identity architecture built on contempt, grievance, or revenge orientation.
inclusion_criteria: Repeated self-framing as permanently wronged
Contempt as stable tone
Revenge fantasies treated as justified essence
Memory of the relationship poisoned in total
exclusion_criteria: Acute grief without contempt identity
Single episode of anger
Clear pain expression without revenge structure
signals.false_positive_risk: Early grief may resemble bitterness before identity hardening is actually present.
signals.escalation_threshold: Escalates to high when bitterness drives speech, child messaging, or logistical decisions.

signals.name: Child Leverage Signal
signals.description: Detected when children are used as emotional carriers, proof, leverage, or symbolic allies in adult rupture.
inclusion_criteria: Using children as messengers
Turning children against the other parent
Seeking validation from children about adult blame
Making children carry adult grief or moral narrative
exclusion_criteria: Age-appropriate explanation to children
Ordinary scheduling communication
Child-centered support that does not load them with adult conflict
signals.false_positive_risk: Necessary communication about the family situation may be confused with leverage when context is thin.
signals.escalation_threshold: Already severe by default; remains critical whenever children are turned into loyalty instruments.

signals.name: Parental Withdrawal Signal
signals.description: Detected when parental intentionality collapses and children disappear from planning or psychological presence.
inclusion_criteria: Children absent from future planning for an extended period
Adult collapse dominates all attention
Language implying total family disappearance rather than couple rupture
exclusion_criteria: Short-term shock with later reorientation
Temporary exhaustion explicitly acknowledged and repaired
Time-limited reduced capacity without abandonment logic
signals.false_positive_risk: Acute survival mode can look like neglect when it is a short shock phase rather than a stable pattern.
signals.escalation_threshold: Escalates to high when withdrawal becomes prolonged, behaviorally entrenched, or combined with active child avoidance.

signals.name: Self-Annihilating Guilt Signal
signals.description: Detected when accountability mutates into total self-erasure, worthlessness logic, or structural self-destruction.
inclusion_criteria: Claims of being unworthy of life or belonging
Totalization of blame onto self without nuance
Guilt language that cancels the right to continue
exclusion_criteria: Concrete remorse without self-erasure
Stable responsibility-taking
Honest regret that still preserves personhood
signals.false_positive_risk: Deep remorse may sound absolute in poetic or crisis language without full self-annihilation structure.
signals.escalation_threshold: Escalates to critical when combined with collapse, suicidal ideation, or inability to function.

signals.name: Administrative Weaponization Signal
signals.description: Detected when law, money, scheduling, housing, paperwork, or logistics are used as disguised punishment channels.
inclusion_criteria: Delaying paperwork to exhaust the other
Using money to humiliate or control
Practical steps designed to maximize pain rather than solve reality
Strategic ambiguity maintained to wear the other down
exclusion_criteria: Normal legal or financial caution
Real disagreement about practical constraints
Necessary boundary-setting without punitive intent
signals.false_positive_risk: Hard but necessary practical decisions may look punitive without evidence of intent or asymmetric cruelty.
signals.escalation_threshold: Escalates to critical if the pattern becomes systematic, repeated, and linked to domination or child harm.

signals.name: Intrusive Interrogation Signal
signals.description: Detected when questioning exceeds ethical boundary and serves domination, injury, or humiliation rather than clarity.
inclusion_criteria: Demanding intimate disclosures to regain control
Obsessive third-party questioning
Interrogation that would only deepen shame or power asymmetry
exclusion_criteria: Necessary practical questions
Boundary clarification questions
Questions tied to safety, money, children, or legal reality
signals.false_positive_risk: Legitimate need for information can be mistaken for intrusion when the context is under-specified.
signals.escalation_threshold: Escalates to high when repeated, humiliating, or linked to surveillance behavior.

signals.name: Residual Goodwill Signal
signals.description: Detected when practical care, mutual easing, or non-possessive goodwill remain active despite rupture.
inclusion_criteria: Helping the other without hidden ownership claim
Making logistics easier for mutual stability
Wishing the other well without control
exclusion_criteria: Kindness used manipulatively to reopen possession
Pseudo-goodwill used to claim moral superiority
Gestures tied to hidden pressure
signals.false_positive_risk: Strategic niceness may mimic goodwill while actually serving manipulation.
signals.escalation_threshold: Positive protective signal; downgraded or nullified if linked to covert coercion.

signals.name: Psychological Collapse Risk Signal
signals.description: Detected when the person shows panic, functional breakdown, or desire not to continue living or coping.
inclusion_criteria: Hyperventilation
Loss of work function
Expressions of wishing to die
Inability to cope with immediate reality
exclusion_criteria: Ordinary sadness
Stable but intense grief without breakdown markers
Poetic intensity without actual collapse indicators
signals.false_positive_risk: Metaphorical or poetic language may exaggerate collapse without indicating immediate risk.
signals.escalation_threshold: Remains critical when self-harm ideation, severe panic, or major functional loss is present.

signals.name: Narrative Absolutism Signal
signals.description: Detected when the story is framed in total innocence versus total guilt, with complexity stripped out.
inclusion_criteria: One-sided blame certainty
No self-implication despite shared history
Villain narrative replacing analysis
Simplistic moral accounting
exclusion_criteria: Clear asymmetry named with evidence
Focused complaint about a concrete harm
Partial narrative explicitly acknowledged as partial
signals.false_positive_risk: A true asymmetrical situation may look absolutist if only a fragment of the case is known.
signals.escalation_threshold: Escalates to high when absolutism drives child leverage, degrading speech, or punishment behavior.

signals.name: Continued Belonging Signal
signals.description: Detected when the person recognizes that family membership and responsibility continue in altered form after rupture.
inclusion_criteria: Acknowledgment of enduring bonds
Commitment to shared responsibility after separation
Language of altered belonging without possession
exclusion_criteria: Possessive claims disguised as belonging
Fusion logic that denies separation
Sentimental denial of boundaries
signals.false_positive_risk: Possessive or boundary-blind language may masquerade as belonging continuity.
signals.escalation_threshold: Positive protective signal; weakened if paired with coercive retention.
